completedActions.name: Add UUID64String branded type to uuid64.ts. EXPORT: type UUID64String = string & { readonly __brand: 'UUID64String' }. This is compile-time only, zero runtime cost. Used by Identifiable and Entity to enforce type-safe id handling. No validation here—validation happens at boundaries (Identifiable.fromString, World.load). No code changes needed, type-only addition.

Verify uuid64.toJSON() behavior. Current: toJSON() returns this.base64 (OPB64 string). This is CORRECT—no changes needed. When JSON.stringify(uuid64Instance) is called, this method returns OPB64 string, not UUID64 object. Verify existing uuid64.test.ts line 250+ covers this behavior.

COMPLETED: Update Identifiable class. (1) Changed private #id: UUID64String → #id: UUID64 (actual POJO). (2) Constructor: constructor(id: UUID64 | string = new UUID64()). Auto-converts string to UUID64 via UUID64.fromString(id) for deserialization support. (3) static fromString(id: string) validates and reconstructs UUID64. (4) Marked static uuid() as @deprecated. Design: UUID64 POJO provides .toTime(), .toBuffer(), .base64 methods. Constructor handles string input from JSON deserialization.
COMPLETED: Update Entity interface. Changed id: string → id: UUID64. Also updated Forma.SCHEMA to use UUID64.avroSchema instead of 'string' for id field.

COMPLETED: Update World.load() method. After JSON.parse(data), reconstruct id as UUID64 POJO using UUID64.fromString(entity.id). Validates id and throws Error if invalid. Returns entity with id as UUID64 POJO.

COMPLETED: Fixed Forma constructor. Identifiable constructor now handles string-to-UUID64 conversion, so Forma passes cfg.id directly to super(). Simplified name generation: removed try/catch, directly calls this.id.timeId() since id is guaranteed UUID64 POJO.

RESOLVE AVRO SERIALIZATION ISSUE: Tests fail with 'invalid UUID64' error from Avro. Root cause: Avro's toBuffer() expects 16-byte fixed buffer but receives UUID64 POJO object. SOLUTION NEEDED: In schema.toAvro() or custom serializer, detect UUID64 objects and extract .uuidv7 buffer for Avro serialization. Current status: 110 tests pass, 15 fail (clock.ts, forma.ts, patch.ts, task.ts avro tests, cli/task.ts). Research POJO/Avro integration: UUID64 POJO has .uuidv7 (Buffer) and .base64 (string) properties. Avro needs the 16-byte buffer for fixed type serialization.
completedActions.test: investigate Avro handling of POJO objects

name: Formalize UUID64 as Entity ID

Tests: uuid64.test.ts (JSON round-trip 250+), identifiable.ts (14-35), forma.ts (23-79), task.ts (28-115)
World storage with id validation needed

summary: Refactor id handling: UUID64 object in memory, OPB64 string in JSON, timeId() for filenames
